html:
[1009]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蛇媒 - Supernatural phenomenon where snakes mediate in romantic affairs, often leading to unexpected or tragic outcomes.
  • 固不諳風月者 - One who is not familiar with romantic affairs, suggesting inexperience or naivety in matters of love.
  • 大澤之側 - Beside a large marsh, referring to a specific location near a body of water.
  • 時屆夏秋交 - When it was the transition between summer and autumn, indicating a specific time of year.
  • 雙蛇長尺余 - Describes two snakes, each about a foot long, emphasizing their size and appearance.
  • 糾纏若饴 - Twisted together like candy, suggesting a tight and intimate embrace.
  • 牢不可開 - Firmly bound and cannot be separated, indicating the strength of their connection.
  • 戲以長策揮之 - Playfully struck them with a long whip, describing the action of the man.
  • 暮歸 - Returned at dusk, indicating the time of day.
  • 夜奔其家 - Ran to his house at night, describing the widow's actions.
  • 百計求合 - Tried every means to consummate the union, indicating her persistence.
  • 事讫各散 - After the matter was completed, they parted ways, indicating the end of the encounter.
  • 私為庆幸 - Privately felt fortunate, suggesting a sense of relief or satisfaction.
  • 素持雅操 - Has always maintained a high moral standard, indicating her previous reputation.
  • 惺然曰 - Awakened and said, indicating a moment of realization.
  • 我何以為此 - Why did I do this? Expressing confusion or regret.
  • 愧而泣 - Felt ashamed and cried, indicating her emotional state.
  • 頓欲自经 - Immediately wanted to hang herself, indicating her intention to commit suicide.
  • 翁姑闻声亟拯之 - The husband and wife heard the sound and quickly rescued her, indicating their reaction.
  • 坚叩其由 - Firmly questioned the reason, indicating their persistence in seeking an explanation.
  • 具以实告 - Told them everything in detail, indicating the full disclosure.
  • 伪以他故假策 - Pretended to have another reason and gave him a whip, indicating a deceptive action.
  • 沸油于镬 - Boiled oil in a pot, describing the method of punishment.
  • 断而投诸其中 - Cut it and threw it into the boiling oil, describing the act of punishment.
  • 肢体糜烂而死 - Died with his limbs melting away, indicating the gruesome outcome.
  • 赍恨亦寻卒 - Carrying her grudge, she soon died, indicating her fate after the incident.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 辽东某县 - A specific county in Liaodong, northeastern China, known for its historical significance and natural beauty.
  • 执鞭 - A person who drives a carriage, often a low-ranking servant or attendant.
  • 比邻之孀 - A widow living nearby, indicating a local and personal connection.
  • 蛇媒 - Supernatural phenomenon in Chinese folklore where snakes are believed to mediate in romantic affairs, often leading to unexpected or tragic outcomes.
  • 夙夜自思 - Thought about it day and night, indicating deep introspection.
  • 赍恨 - Carrying a grudge, indicating a lasting emotional burden.

Proper Nouns

  • 辽东某县 - A specific county in Liaodong, northeastern China.
  • 先大父 - Refers to the narrator's grandfather, a respected family member.
  • 执鞭 - A person who drives a carriage, often a low-ranking servant or attendant.
  • 比邻之孀 - A widow living nearby, indicating a local and personal connection.

[1010]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A traditional phrase used by historians or commentators to introduce their own commentary or reflection.
  • 不知而误用，犹亡其身 - A proverbial expression meaning 'to use something without knowing it, one still risks losing oneself.'
  • 况知而故用者哉 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how much more so if one knowingly and deliberately uses it.'
  • 此事先大父谆谆言之 - Means 'my grandfather repeatedly spoke of this matter,' emphasizing the importance of the message.
  • 用以戒人 - Means 'to warn others with it,' indicating the purpose of the grandfather's words.
  • 予小子弗敢忘也 - A humble expression meaning 'I, the younger one, dare not forget,' showing respect and commitment to remembering the lesson.
  • 慎之！慎之！ - A repeated exclamation meaning 'be cautious! Be cautious!' for emphasis.
  • 其勿以余为导淫而妄思一试 - Means 'do not think that I am leading you into debauchery and rashly try it,' warning against misinterpreting the advice.
  • 则幸矣 - Means 'then you will be fortunate,' expressing a wish for the reader's well-being.
  • 续五通 - A heading that suggests a continuation or additional information about '五通' (Wu Tong), a folk deity.
  • 五通邪神 - Refers to the Wu Tong deity, who is considered a malevolent spirit in Chinese folklore.
  • 近今奉祀者少减 - Means 'in recent times, those who worship it have decreased,' indicating a decline in the deity's popularity.
  • 然而旧闻犹有可嗤者 - Means 'however, old stories still have some laughable elements,' suggesting that there are amusing or absurd aspects to the tales.
  • 因并附录于左 - Means 'therefore, I will append them below,' indicating that the author will include these stories.
  • 故明天顺中 - Means 'in the Ming dynasty during the Tian Shun period,' providing a specific historical context.
  • 钱塘民戴小一 - Refers to a man named Dai Xiaoyi from Qiantang (modern-day Hangzhou).
  • 鄙而有力 - Means 'humble but strong,' describing Dai Xiaoyi's character.
  • 其室某氏 - Refers to his wife, whose name is not specified.
  • 虽村女颇具冶容 - Means 'although she was a village girl, she had a very attractive appearance,' describing her beauty.
  • 齿且甚稚 - Means 'her teeth were very young,' indicating that she was very young.
  • 小一防闲綦密 - Means 'Dai Xiaoyi kept a very tight watch,' emphasizing his vigilance.
  • 人固无能问鼎 - Means 'no one could possibly interfere,' indicating the difficulty of anyone breaking into their home.
  • 即妇亦未敢自炫其玉 - Means 'even his wife did not dare to flaunt her beauty,' showing the strictness of their relationship.
  • 一夕，夫妇已就寝 - Means 'one night, after the couple had gone to bed,' setting the scene.
  • 忽闻窗外呵殿声 - Means 'suddenly, they heard the sound of a carriage outside the window,' describing the unusual noise.
  • 即有人叱曰 - Means 'then someone shouted,' introducing the voice that spoke.
  • 戴小一何物小人，神圣过而犹然拥妻酣卧也 - Means 'Dai Xiaoyi, what kind of lowly person are you, allowing a holy being to pass by while you are still sleeping with your wife,' criticizing Dai Xiaoyi's behavior.
  • 小一大惊 - Means 'Dai Xiaoyi was greatly startled,' showing his reaction.
  • 穴牖窥之 - Means 'he peeped through a hole in the window,' describing his action.
  • 纱笼十数对，卤薄纷沓中拥一贵人 - Means 'there were several pairs of lanterns, and in the midst of the entourage, a noble person was being carried,' describing the scene.
  • 紫衣而金冠，跨小骊驹 - Means 'wearing purple clothes and a golden crown, riding a small black horse,' describing the appearance of the noble person.
  • 盖村中所祀五郎，即神之二也 - Means 'it was the Fifth Lord worshipped in the village, who is the second of the Wu Tong deities,' identifying the deity.
  • 村人素畏此神 - Means 'the villagers always feared this god,' emphasizing the deity's power.
  • 小一蹶然起视 - Means 'Dai Xiaoyi jumped up and looked,' showing his reaction to seeing the deity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A term used by historians or commentators to introduce their own reflections.
  • 五通 - A folk deity in Chinese mythology, often considered malevolent.
  • 聊斋 - Refers to the *Liaozhai Zhiyi* (Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio), a famous collection of ghost and demon stories by Pu Songling.
  • 天顺 - A reign title of Emperor Yingzong of the Ming dynasty (1457-1464).
  • 钱塘 - An ancient name for Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 五郎 - One of the five deities in the Wu Tong pantheon, often associated with wealth and fertility.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - A term used by historians or commentators.
  • 五通 - A folk deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 聊斋 - *Liaozhai Zhiyi* (Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio) by Pu Songling.
  • 天顺 - A reign title of Emperor Yingzong of the Ming dynasty.
  • 钱塘 - An ancient name for Hangzhou.
  • 五郎 - One of the five deities in the Wu Tong pantheon.

[1011]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 婦則已熟寐 - Literally 'the wife is already sound asleep,' used to describe the wife's state.
  • 將呼之醒同往礼拜 - Means 'to call her awake and go to worship together.'
  • 神忽隔窗止之曰 - Means 'the deity suddenly stopped them from the window and said.'
  • 勿惊丽人 - Means 'do not startle the beautiful woman.'
  • 予之来亦正为此 - Means 'I have come for this very reason.'
  • 小一性素妒 - Means 'Xiao Yi has always been jealous.'
  • 闻神言颇不能平 - Means 'hearing the deity's words, he was greatly unsettled.'
  • 且知近村之妇，多有为神蹂者 - Means 'and knowing that many women in the nearby village have been trampled by the deity.'
  • 奋然不顾 - Means 'bravely without hesitation.'
  • 淫鬼耳，何足为神 - Means 'it's just a lustful ghost, what kind of deity is it?'
  • 其真能加祸于予哉 - Means 'could it really bring disaster upon me?'
  • 径弛然复卧 - Means 'he lay down relaxed and went back to sleep.'
  • 若不知有神者 - Means 'as if he did not know there was a deity.'
  • 外又呼之 - Means 'someone else called him.'
  • 小一漫应之曰 - Means 'Xiao Yi replied carelessly.'
  • 予已寝矣，神何为者 - Means 'I am already asleep, what does the deity want?'
  • 予妇恐不若他妇之易致也 - Means 'my wife is probably not as easy to seduce as other women.'
  • 语未竟，神即哂曰 - Means 'before he finished speaking, the deity smiled and said.'
  • 予固谓渠倔强，非可以理化者 - Means 'I had indeed thought he was stubborn and could not be reasoned with.'
  • 汝且姑待之 - Means 'you may wait for him for now.'
  • 乃叱其从者，行如风雨，疾驰而去 - Means 'then he shouted at his followers, who left as fast as wind and rain.'
  • 小一始撼妇醒 - Means 'Xiao Yi finally shook his wife awake.'
  • 语之，妇大怖 - Means 'told her, and the wife was greatly frightened.'
  • 小二笑曰 - Means 'Xiao Er laughed and said.'
  • 予有力如虎 - Means 'I have strength like a tiger.'
  • 神即能亦无如我何 - Means 'even if the deity could, it would be of no use against me.'
  • 子勿以为忧 - Means 'do not worry about it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 神 - Refers to a deity or supernatural being.
  • 小一 - Refers to Xiao Yi, a character in the story.
  • 小二 - Refers to Xiao Er, another character in the story.
  • 近村之妇 - Refers to women in the nearby village who have been affected by the deity.
  • 淫鬼 - Refers to a lustful ghost or deity.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence introduces the setting and the characters' initial actions.
  • This sentence describes the deity's intervention.
  • This sentence explains the deity's purpose.
  • This sentence describes Xiao Yi's reaction to the deity's words.
  • This sentence provides background information about the deity's actions.
  • This sentence describes Xiao Yi's defiant response.
  • This sentence expresses Xiao Yi's skepticism about the deity's power.
  • This sentence describes Xiao Yi's dismissive attitude.
  • This sentence describes another person calling Xiao Yi.
  • This sentence describes Xiao Yi's response to the call.
  • This sentence describes the deity's reaction to Xiao Yi's words.
  • This sentence explains the deity's assessment of Xiao Yi.
  • This sentence describes the deity's departure.
  • This sentence describes Xiao Yi's actions after the deity leaves.
  • This sentence introduces Xiao Er's response.
  • This sentence describes Xiao Er's reassurance to his wife.

Proper Nouns

  • 小一 - Xiao Yi
  • 小二 - Xiao Er

[1012]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明日 - Refers to the next day.
  • 小一 - A name, likely a common name or nickname.
  • 出田间 - Means 'went to the fields.'
  • 心系其妇 - Means 'was preoccupied with his wife.'
  • 往返数四 - Means 'went back and forth several times.'
  • 妇故无恙也 - Means 'the wife was still well.'
  • 里人莫知其由 - Means 'the villagers did not know the reason.'
  • 咸戏曰 - Means 'all joked and said.'
  • 若今日足趾何勤也 - Means 'Why are you so diligent today?'
  • 岂阿嫂亦紧待播种耶 - Means 'Is your sister-in-law also eagerly waiting for sowing?'
  • 小一怍不能言 - Means 'Xiao Yi was embarrassed and could not speak.'
  • 及昏而归 - Means 'until evening when he returned.'
  • 与妇谋所以备御之策 - Means 'and with his wife, they discussed strategies to prepare for defense.'
  • 乃以巨石镇其户 - Means 'they placed a large stone to block the door.'
  • 令妇戒严而卧 - Means 'they ordered the wife to be on high alert and sleep.'
  • 衣裤皆亲以密线缀之 - Means 'clothes and pants were all sewn tightly with fine threads.'
  • 室中不燃炬 - Means 'there was no light in the room.'
  • 而自荷一锹 - Means 'and carried a shovel himself.'
  • 摩厉以须 - Means 'sharpened it in preparation.'
  • 其防维可谓密已 - Means 'their defense measures can be said to be very thorough.'
  • 如是者三夕 - Means 'like this for three nights.'
  • 而竟杳然 - Means 'but still nothing happened.'
  • 妇亦渐厌其为 - Means 'the wife also gradually grew tired of it.'
  • 唾曰 - Means 'spat out and said.'
  • 岂若梦魇而妄有所见耶 - Means 'Could it be that I am having a nightmare and seeing things that aren't real?'
  • 脱令有之 - Means 'If there is indeed something.'
  • 宁以神之威灵 - Means 'Even with the power of the spirits.'
  • 而遂惮此不来者 - Means 'they would still be afraid to come.'
  • 小一终不释然 - Means 'Xiao Yi was still not at ease.'
  • 仍前戒备 - Means 'still maintained vigilance.'
  • 夜未深而神果至 - Means 'before midnight, the spirit indeed arrived.'
  • 其来也声势远逊于前 - Means 'the sound and appearance were much less than before.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明日 - Refers to the next day, a common time reference in classical Chinese.
  • 小一 - A name, likely a common name or nickname.
  • 田间 - Refers to the fields, indicating agricultural work.
  • 心系其妇 - Indicates a preoccupation with one's spouse, reflecting traditional family values.
  • 里人 - Refers to the villagers, indicating a community setting.
  • 戏曰 - Indicates a playful or teasing tone, common in social interactions.
  • 阿嫂 - Refers to a sister-in-law, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 播种 - Refers to planting seeds, a common agricultural activity.
  • 巨石镇其户 - Refers to using a large stone to block the door, indicating a defensive measure.
  • 戒严 - Refers to being on high alert, indicating a state of readiness.
  • 衣裤皆亲以密线缀之 - Refers to sewing clothes and pants tightly with fine threads, indicating careful preparation.
  • 室中不燃炬 - Refers to no light in the room, indicating darkness and secrecy.
  • 荷一锹 - Refers to carrying a shovel, indicating a defensive tool.
  • 摩厉以须 - Refers to sharpening something in preparation, indicating readiness.
  • 防维 - Refers to defense measures, indicating preparedness.
  • 杳然 - Refers to nothing happening, indicating a lack of occurrence.
  • 梦魇 - Refers to a nightmare, indicating a disturbed state of mind.
  • 神之威灵 - Refers to the power of the spirits, indicating supernatural belief.
  • 释然 - Refers to being at ease, indicating a state of calm.
  • 夜未深 - Refers to before midnight, indicating a specific time of day.
  • 声势远逊于前 - Refers to the sound and appearance being much less than before, indicating a change in intensity.

Proper Nouns

  • 小一 - A name, likely a common name or nickname.
  • 阿嫂 - Refers to a sister-in-law, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 播种 - Refers to planting seeds, a common agricultural activity.
  • 巨石 - Refers to a large stone, indicating a defensive measure.
  • 牖 - Refers to windows, indicating a defensive measure.
  • 梦魇 - Refers to a nightmare, indicating a disturbed state of mind.
  • 神之威灵 - Refers to the power of the spirits, indicating supernatural belief.
  • 释然 - Refers to being at ease, indicating a state of calm.
  • 夜未深 - Refers to before midnight, indicating a specific time of day.
  • 声势远逊于前 - Refers to the sound and appearance being much less than before, indicating a change in intensity.

[1013]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惟闻 - Means 'only heard' or 'heard only,' indicating a limited or partial perception.
  • 篱落间 - Refers to the space between fences or hedges, often used to describe a rural or secluded setting.
  • 踯躅 - Describes a hesitant or shuffling gait, often used to depict a sense of uncertainty or fear.
  • 所乘之驹 - Means 'the horse he was riding,' where '驹' specifically refers to a young horse.
  • 小一心知有异 - Means 'Xiao Yi knew there was something unusual,' indicating a sense of suspicion or awareness.
  • 蹴妇便起 - Means 'kicked his wife to get up,' using '蹴' to describe a forceful action.
  • 来矣！卧必不免 - Means 'Here it comes! We can't escape if we stay here,' expressing a sense of urgency and danger.
  • 悚然 - Describes a state of being startled or terrified.
  • 毛发尽矗 - Means 'hair standing on end,' a vivid description of extreme fear.
  • 手足亦无所措 - Means 'hands and feet have no place to go,' indicating a state of panic and helplessness.
  • 未几 - Means 'in a short while' or 'soon after.'
  • 猛风大作 - Means 'a strong wind suddenly arose,' describing a sudden and intense natural event.
  • 瓦砾纷飞 - Means 'tiles and debris flying everywhere,' depicting the destructive force of the wind.
  • 应门者自移而司闭者自启 - Means 'the person who answers the door moves on their own, and the person who locks the door opens it on their own,' suggesting a chaotic and uncontrollable situation.
  • 户牖洞开 - Means 'doors and windows wide open,' indicating a state of complete openness and exposure.
  • 向所为闭关谢客者，又俨若开门揖盗 - Means 'what was once closed off to visitors now looks like it's inviting thieves,' expressing a sense of irony and vulnerability.
  • 木立而视 - Means 'stood rooted in place and looked,' describing a state of shock and paralysis.
  • 顿忘利器在身 - Means 'suddenly forgot the sharp tools he had on him,' indicating a momentary lapse in awareness.
  • 反束手以待之 - Means 'instead of taking action, he stood with his hands tied behind his back,' suggesting a state of helplessness.
  • 俄而烛光自外而内 - Means 'soon after, the candlelight came from outside to inside,' describing a sudden change in the environment.
  • 衾枕易故以新 - Means 'the bedclothes and pillows were replaced with new ones,' indicating a change in the setting.
  • 室中之什物须臾一空 - Means 'the household items in the room disappeared in an instant,' describing a rapid and complete change.
  • 锹固不可得而知也 - Means 'the spade, however, cannot be known,' indicating that something remains unknown or unexplained.
  • 神犹未入 - Means 'the spirit has not yet entered,' suggesting a state of limbo or uncertainty.
  • 捭者复阖 - Means 'the one who opens closes again,' describing a cyclical or repetitive action.
  • 弭缝者既以不解解之 - Means 'the seams of the clothes were undone,' indicating a state of disarray or disorganization.
  • 裤之联络者又以不断断之 - Means 'the connections of the pants were broken,' further emphasizing the state of disarray.
  • 俄顷为无叶之花 - Means 'in a moment, it became a flower without leaves,' describing a sudden and dramatic change.
  • 转盼非在椟之玉 - Means 'in a blink, it was no longer a jade in the box,' suggesting a loss or transformation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 篱落间 - Refers to a rural or secluded setting, often used in classical literature to evoke a sense of tranquility or isolation.
  • 踯躅 - A term often used to describe a hesitant or fearful gait, commonly found in classical poetry and prose.
  • 所乘之驹 - Refers to a young horse, which in classical literature often symbolizes youth, vitality, or a journey.
  • 小一 - A name that may refer to a historical figure or a fictional character, but without additional context, it is difficult to determine its exact significance.
  • 应门者自移而司闭者自启 - Refers to the chaotic and uncontrollable nature of a sudden event, often used to describe the disruption of social order.
  • 户牖洞开 - Refers to the vulnerability and exposure of a household, often used to describe a state of danger or threat.
  • 向所为闭关谢客者，又俨若开门揖盗 - Refers to the irony of a situation where what was once secure becomes vulnerable, often used to describe a reversal of fortune.
  • 木立而视 - Refers to a state of shock and paralysis, often used to describe the reaction to a sudden and unexpected event.
  • 顿忘利器在身 - Refers to a momentary lapse in awareness, often used to describe a state of confusion or distraction.
  • 反束手以待之 - Refers to a state of helplessness, often used to describe the inability to take action in the face of danger.
  • 俄而烛光自外而内 - Refers to a sudden change in the environment, often used to describe the arrival of a new element or the resolution of a situation.
  • 衾枕易故以新 - Refers to a change in the setting, often used to describe the transition from one state to another.
  • 室中之什物须臾一空 - Refers to a rapid and complete change, often used to describe the disappearance of possessions or the loss of a familiar environment.
  • 锹固不可得而知也 - Refers to something that remains unknown or unexplained, often used to describe the limits of human knowledge or understanding.
  • 神犹未入 - Refers to a state of limbo or uncertainty, often used to describe the transition between life and death or the unknown.
  • 捭者复阖 - Refers to a cyclical or repetitive action, often used to describe the natural cycles of life or the repetitive nature of certain events.
  • 弭缝者既以不解解之 - Refers to a state of disarray or disorganization, often used to describe the breakdown of order or the loss of control.
  • 裤之联络者又以不断断之 - Refers to the breakdown of connections, often used to describe the loss of relationships or the disintegration of a system.
  • 俄顷为无叶之花 - Refers to a sudden and dramatic change, often used to describe the transformation of a situation or the loss of something valuable.
  • 转盼非在椟之玉 - Refers to a loss or transformation, often used to describe the disappearance of something precious or the change of a situation.

[1014]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不禁心灰气绝已 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'cannot help but feel utterly hopeless.'
  • 神始含笑入室 - Vivid description of a deity or spirit entering a room with a smile, suggesting a change in demeanor.
  • 裘带翩翩 - Poetic description of flowing, graceful robes and sashes.
  • 若妇诚不易致也 - Statement meaning 'this woman is indeed difficult to attain.'
  • 盍牵去之 - Command meaning 'why don't you take her away?'
  • 卧榻之侧不宜有此癱人 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'it is inappropriate for such a person to be near the bed.'
  • 零云断雨之声渐达于外 - Poetic description of sounds of lovemaking becoming audible.
  • 荡然情状 - Term meaning 'a state of unrestrained passion.'
  • 胆力益怯 - Phrase meaning 'courage and strength diminish further.'
  • 青燐隐隐 - Vivid description of faint blue light, often associated with ghosts or spirits.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 神 - Refers to a deity or spirit, often associated with supernatural beings in Chinese mythology.
  • 妇 - Refers to a woman, often in a marital context.
  • 癱人 - Term that can be interpreted as a disabled person or someone who is weak and helpless.
  • 青燐 - Refers to phosphorescence, often associated with ghosts or spirits in Chinese folklore.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '小一于此，不禁心灰气绝已' is elliptical and can be interpreted as 'Small One here, cannot help but feel utterly hopeless.'
  • The clause '神始含笑入室，裘带翩翩' describes the deity's entry into the room, with a change in demeanor and appearance.
  • The clause '若妇诚不易致也' is a statement about the difficulty of attaining the woman.
  • The command '盍牵去之' is directed at someone to take the woman away.
  • The metaphorical expression '卧榻之侧不宜有此癱人' is used to describe the inappropriate presence of a weak or helpless person near the bed.
  • The poetic description '零云断雨之声渐达于外' is used to convey the sounds of lovemaking becoming audible.
  • The term '荡然情状' is used to describe a state of unrestrained passion.
  • The phrase '胆力益怯' describes a further decrease in courage and strength.
  • The vivid description '青燐隐隐' is used to convey the presence of faint blue light, often associated with ghosts or spirits.
  • The metaphors '饭牛牧羊' are used to describe humble, ordinary tasks.

[1015]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幸燐火尽息 - Fortunately, the phosphorus fire has finally gone out.
  • 妖物稍远 - The strange creatures have slightly retreated.
  • 因筹所以同谋者 - Thus, he thought about those who had conspired with him.
  • 邻比皆素畏此神 - The neighbors all have long been afraid of this god.
  • 左侧一老教授颇饶于胆 - A venerable professor on the left side is quite bold.
  • 且居恒时诋五通 - And he often criticizes the Wutong.
  • 或可借箸于彼 - Perhaps we can rely on him.
  • 不敢由门出，惧为神知 - He dared not go out through the door, fearing that the god would know.
  • 遂皔垣而就之 - He thus climbed over the wall to approach him.
  • 适犹来寝 - He happened to be sleeping.
  • 款关而入 - Knocked on the door and entered.
  • 方孑然一身坐于短檠下 - He was sitting alone under a short lamp.
  • 整理残编 - Tidying up the remnants of a manuscript.
  • 见其突如 - Seeing him suddenly.
  • 不禁诧异 - Could not help but be surprised.
  • 亟起而询之 - Quickly stood up to ask him.
  • 小一呐呐以告，且言其详 - Little One hesitantly reported and explained in detail.
  • 教授色然怒曰 - The professor angrily said.
  • 以神而行秽，其若人何 - If a god acts so foully, how can it be tolerated.
  • 予固恶之久矣 - I have hated it for a long time.
  • 请与子偕往，为子面叱而退之 - I will go with you and confront and drive it away.
  • 小一犹豫未信 - Little One hesitated and did not believe.
  • 教授据操戒方欲行 - The professor took his weapons and was about to leave.
  • 子勿虑，不从则打之，谅渠亦无能相抗 - Do not worry, if it does not comply, we will beat it, it cannot resist.
  • 小一不得已，遂偕来 - Little One had no choice but to accompany him.
  • 仍骑墙而过 - They still climbed over the wall.
  • 甫及户侧，已闻室中神语曰 - Just as they reached the side of the house, they heard the god speaking inside.
  • 此老至，吾当谨避之 - This old man is coming, I will carefully avoid him.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妖物 - Refers to strange or supernatural creatures, often with negative connotations.
  • 五通 - Refers to the Wutong, a deity or spirit known for protection and sometimes mischievous behavior, often associated with the Five Peaks of Mount Wutai.
  • 借箸 - A metaphorical expression for seeking assistance, derived from the story of Zhang Liang borrowing a spoon to advise Liu Bang.
  • 短檠 - Refers to a small lamp or candlestick, commonly used in scholarly settings.
  • 残编 - Refers to the remnants of a manuscript, often used to describe incomplete or damaged texts.
  • 神语 - Refers to the speech of a god or supernatural entity, often with a sense of awe or fear.
  • 户侧 - Refers to the side of a house or door, often used to describe a location near the entrance.

Proper Nouns

  • 五通 - The Wutong, a deity or spirit known for protection and sometimes mischievous behavior.
  • 短檠 - A small lamp or candlestick, commonly used in scholarly settings.
  • 残编 - The remnants of a manuscript, often used to describe incomplete or damaged texts.
  • 神语 - The speech of a god or supernatural entity, often with a sense of awe or fear.
  • 户侧 - The side of a house or door, often used to describe a location near the entrance.

[1016]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不然 - Negation phrase meaning 'if not' or 'otherwise,' used to introduce a counterfactual or alternative scenario.
  • 血食 - Term that can refer to offerings of meat or sacrifices, often used metaphorically in religious or ritual contexts.
  • 胥 - Term that can mean 'all' or 'everyone,' often used to emphasize a collective or universal condition.
  • 不得享矣 - Phrase meaning 'will not be able to enjoy' or 'will not be able to partake,' indicating a loss of privilege or status.
  • 二郎速出见我 - Command to 'Second Brother, come out and see me,' addressing a deity or figure of authority.
  • 人头畜鸣者 - Derogatory term meaning 'a person with the head of a human but the voice of an animal,' used to criticize someone for lacking human qualities.
  • 逡巡 - Term meaning 'hesitantly' or 'timidly,' describing cautious or reluctant movement.
  • 匍匐 - Term meaning 'to crawl' or 'to prostrate oneself,' often used to show submission or humility.
  • 请罪 - Term meaning 'to ask for forgiveness' or 'to confess guilt,' indicating a formal act of apology.
  • 一方保障 - Term meaning 'a protector of one region' or 'a guardian of one area,' referring to a deity or figure with protective powers.
  • 污部民之妻 - Term meaning 'to defile the wife of a commoner,' indicating an act of moral transgression.
  • 怙势宣淫 - Term meaning 'to abuse power to indulge in lust,' criticizing someone for using their authority for immoral purposes.
  • 无所忌惮 - Term meaning 'without any fear or shame,' describing someone who acts without regard for consequences.
  • 笔刀不利 - Term meaning 'the pen and knife are not sharp,' used metaphorically to suggest that one's words or actions lack power or effectiveness.
  • 九阍 - Term referring to the nine gates of heaven, symbolizing the highest authority or divine court.
  • 十地 - Term referring to the ten directions or realms, symbolizing the entire universe or all possible places.
  • 尊荣不享 - Term meaning 'will not enjoy honor and respect,' indicating a loss of status or dignity.
  • 庙貌无存 - Term meaning 'the temple and image will not remain,' indicating the destruction or loss of a place of worship.
  • 除神藉而落鬼道 - Term meaning 'to be demoted from a deity to a ghost,' indicating a severe punishment or demotion in the afterlife.
  • 顿首至地 - Term meaning 'to bow repeatedly until touching the ground,' a formal act of deep respect or submission.
  • 唯唯而已 - Term meaning 'just nodding in agreement,' indicating a lack of further response or argument.
  • 重警 - Term meaning 'a serious warning,' indicating a severe caution or threat.
  • 无杖可以叩胫 - Term meaning 'no stick to tap the shins,' a metaphor for lacking the means to punish physically.
  • 夏楚 - Term referring to a type of cane or whip used for corporal punishment.
  • 聊以示威 - Term meaning 'just to show authority,' indicating a symbolic act of punishment.
  • 嘶痛 - Term meaning 'to groan in pain,' describing the sound of someone in distress.
  • 片言 - Term meaning 'a single word' or 'a brief statement,' indicating a lack of further communication.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 二郎 - Refers to the deity Erlang Shen, a prominent figure in Chinese mythology known for his role as a protector and judge.
  • 教授 - Refers to a scholar or teacher, often with a high degree of respect and authority.
  • 九阍 - Refers to the nine gates of heaven, a concept from Chinese mythology and cosmology.
  • 十地 - Refers to the ten directions or realms, a concept from Chinese cosmology and Buddhism.
  • 庙貌 - Refers to the temple and image of a deity, indicating a place of worship.
  • 神藉 - Refers to the status or position of a deity, indicating a high rank in the divine hierarchy.
  • 鬼道 - Refers to the realm of ghosts, indicating a lower status in the afterlife.

Proper Nouns

  • 二郎 - The deity Erlang Shen.
  • 教授 - A scholar or teacher.
  • 九阍 - The nine gates of heaven.
  • 十地 - The ten directions or realms.
  • 庙貌 - The temple and image of a deity.
  • 神藉 - The status or position of a deity.
  • 鬼道 - The realm of ghosts.

[1017]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 教授杖讫 - Professor struck the end or finished punishing; '杖' (zhàng) means 'to strike' or 'to whip', often used in a disciplinary context.
  • 念汝居位，稍从轻刑 - Considering your position, I have slightly reduced the punishment; '念' (niàn) means 'considering' or 'thinking about', and '稍从轻刑' (shāo cóng qīng xíng) means 'slightly reduced the punishment'.
  • 此亦周官议贵之典也 - This is also in line with the Zhou official's regulations for nobles; '周官' (zhōu guān) refers to the officials of the Zhou dynasty, and '议贵' (yì guì) means 'regulations for nobles'.
  • 可速去，再犯当不宥 - You can leave quickly, but if you commit the offense again, there will be no forgiveness; '宥' (yòu) means 'forgiveness' or 'pardon'.
  • 神又唯唯，忽遂不见 - The spirit nodded again and suddenly disappeared; '唯唯' (wéi wéi) means 'nodding' or 'agreeing'.
  • 小一究不解其故，请于教授 - Xiao Yi could not understand the reason and asked the professor; '究' (jiū) means 'to investigate' or 'to understand'.
  • 微晒曰 - Smiled slightly and said; '微晒' (wēi shài) means 'smiled slightly'.
  • 此非子所能知也 - This is not something you can know; '子' (zǐ) is a respectful term for 'you'.
  • 渠岂畏我老书生？畏我浩然之气耳 - Does he fear me, an old scholar? He fears my righteous energy; '渠' (qú) means 'he', '老书生' (lǎo shū shēng) means 'old scholar', and '浩然之气' (hào rán zhī qì) means 'righteous energy'.
  • 子可入视尊阃，事非得已，琴瑟仍宜如前，毋以一眚而弃永好 - You can go inside and see your wife. It is not by choice, so the music and harmony should remain as before. Do not abandon the eternal good because of one mistake; '尊阃' (zūn kǔn) means 'your wife', '琴瑟' (qín sè) refers to marital harmony, '一眚' (yī shěng) means 'one mistake', and '永好' (yǒng hǎo) means 'eternal good'.
  • 予亦行矣 - I am also leaving; '予' (yǔ) means 'I' or 'me'.
  • 遂别去 - Then left; '别去' (bié qù) means 'to leave'.
  • 小一入室，诸物又皆珠还 - Xiao Yi entered the room, and all the objects returned as if by magic; '珠还' (zhū huán) means 'to return as if by magic'.
  • 惟妇白身偃卧，形状如痴，以汤灌之始甦 - Only the wife lay motionless, her shape like that of a fool, and she only woke up after being bathed with warm water; '白身' (bái shēn) means 'motionless', '偃卧' (yǎn wò) means 'lying down', '形状如痴' (xíng zhuàng rú chī) means 'shape like a fool', and '以汤灌之始甦' (yǐ tāng guàn zhī shǐ sū) means 'only woke up after being bathed with warm water'.
  • 砌下泥皮盈地，即神所受杖处也 - The ground was covered with mud, which was where the spirit was struck; '砌下' (qì xià) means 'under the wall', '泥皮' (ní pí) means 'mud', '盈地' (yíng dì) means 'covered the ground', and '即神所受杖处也' (jí shén suǒ shòu zhàng chù yě) means 'which was where the spirit was struck'.
  • 往谢教授，生徒对曰 - Xiao Yi went to thank the professor, and the students replied; '生徒' (shēng tú) means 'students'.
  • 师于昧爽即束装启行，我辈后至者犹未得见，实不知其焉往 - The teacher left early in the morning, already packed and ready to go. We who arrived later still did not see him, and we do not know where he went; '昧爽' (mèi shuǎng) means 'dawn', '束装启行' (shù zhuāng qǐ xíng) means 'packed and ready to go', '我辈后至者' (wǒ bèi hòu zhì zhě) means 'we who arrived later', '犹未得见' (yóu wèi dé jiàn) means 'still did not see him', and '实不知其焉往' (shí bù zhī qí yān wǎng) means 'do not know where he went'.
  • 复趋五通祠，私入覸之 - He then hurried to the Five-Passage Temple and secretly entered to see; '五通祠' (wǔ tōng cí) means 'Five-Passage Temple', '私入覸之' (sī rù jiàn zhī) means 'secretly entered to see'.
  • 其次坐者自腰以下颜色剥落数片，余俱如旧 - The one sitting next to him had several patches of his lower body peeling off, while the rest remained unchanged; '次坐者' (cì zuò zhě) means 'the one sitting next to him', '自腰以下' (zì yāo yǐ xià) means 'from the waist down', '颜色剥落' (yán sè bāo luò) means 'peeling off', '数片' (shù piàn) means 'several patches', and '余俱如旧' (yú jù rú jiù) means 'while the rest remained unchanged'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 教授 - A title for a teacher or scholar.
  • 周官 - Refers to the officials of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 五通祠 - A temple dedicated to the Five-Passage deities, often associated with local folklore and supernatural beings.
  • 浩然之气 - A concept from Mencius, referring to a noble and righteous spirit.

Proper Nouns

  • 教授 - A title for a teacher or scholar.
  • 五通祠 - A temple dedicated to the Five-Passage deities.

[1018]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A conventional phrase used to introduce a commentary or reflection, often by a historian or chronicler.
  • 素以力闻 - Literally 'known for strength,' referring to someone renowned for their physical prowess.
  • 气馁于神 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to lose courage in the face of spirit' or 'to be intimidated by the supernatural.'
  • 奇梃以击 - A phrase describing an action involving a large stick or club used to strike or defend oneself.
  • 究不免 - Means 'ultimately cannot avoid' or 'finally could not escape.'
  • 使当时奇梃以击，未必遂为所污 - A conditional statement suggesting that if the person had used a large stick to strike, they might not have been defiled.
  • 何其临事而惧也 - A rhetorical question expressing surprise or criticism at someone's fearfulness in a critical situation.
  • 教授侃侃正言 - Describes a teacher speaking confidently and clearly, emphasizing their boldness and integrity.
  • 勇怯实由于养 - A philosophical statement meaning 'courage and cowardice truly depend on cultivation' or 'are determined by one's moral and mental training.'
  • 老壮弗与焉 - A phrase indicating that age or physical strength does not determine courage or cowardice.
  • 第传者欲神其事 - A statement suggesting that the storyteller or historian wanted to make the event seem more miraculous or extraordinary.
  • 仿丈人之行 - Refers to imitating the actions of a respected elder or mentor.
  • 薄责不足示惩 - Means 'light punishment is insufficient as a deterrent' or 'mild retribution is not enough to serve as a warning.'
  • 遂复有罹其荼毒者 - Means 'and thus others suffered under the same harsh treatment.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A title used for a historian or chronicler, often in a satirical or critical context.
  • 教授 - Refers to a teacher or scholar, often in the context of Confucian education.
  • 丈人 - A respectful term for an elder or mentor, often used in the context of Confucian ethics.
  • 勇怯 - Refers to the concepts of courage and cowardice, which are central to Confucian and Daoist thought.
  • 养 - Refers to moral and mental cultivation, a key concept in Confucianism.
  • 老壮 - Refers to the dichotomy of age and physical strength, contrasting with the idea that courage is not determined by these factors.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - A title used for a historian or chronicler.
  • 教授 - A title for a teacher or scholar.
  • 丈人 - A respectful term for an elder or mentor.

[1019]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 康熙初年 - Refers to the early years of the Kangxi era (1662–1722), a period of significant cultural and political development in Qing China.
  • 改醮之謀 - A euphemism for the plan to remarry, particularly in the context of a widow.
  • 歸寧其母家 - A formal expression meaning 'to visit her mother's home,' often used to describe a daughter-in-law visiting her in-laws.
  • 喁喁獨往 - Describes the woman's solitary departure, with '喁喁' conveying a sense of quiet or subdued emotion.
  • 足甫及門 - Means 'her feet had just reached the door,' indicating a moment of hesitation or pause.
  • 敝衣褴褛 - A vivid description of tattered or worn-out clothing, emphasizing the beggar's poverty.
  • 眇而跛一足 - Describes a beggar who is both blind in one eye and lame in one foot.
  • 相視而笑，意颇涉邪 - The beggar and the woman exchange a glance and smile, with the woman interpreting it as having a 'sleazy' or 'impure' intent.
  • 隱忍自去 - Means 'to endure silently and leave,' reflecting the woman's restraint in the face of discomfort.
  • 苦无岐路可避 - Describes the woman's distress at having no alternative path to escape.
  • 自恃白昼，无敢谁何 - The woman relies on the safety of daylight, believing that no one would dare to harm her.
  • 重过其地 - Means 'to pass by the same place again,' indicating a return to the scene of earlier events.
  • 鹑衣百结 - A metaphor for extremely ragged clothing, emphasizing the beggars' poverty.
  • 大恐 - Means 'greatly frightened,' conveying the woman's intense fear.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 康熙初年 - The early Kangxi period, a time of relative stability and cultural flourishing after the Qing conquest of China.
  • 五通祠 - A shrine dedicated to the Five Lords, deities associated with fertility, wealth, and protection in Chinese folk religion.
  • 淫雨倾圮 - Describes heavy rain causing the shrine to collapse, symbolizing the decay of traditional structures.
  • 神象亦都破坏 - Indicates that the statues or images of the deities have also been destroyed, further emphasizing the shrine's decline.
  • 里中方鸠工 - Means 'the villagers are currently gathering workers,' suggesting a community effort to rebuild the shrine.
  • 丐者 - Refers to beggars, who are often associated with poverty and marginalization in Chinese society.
  • 白昼 - Refers to daylight, which is often contrasted with nighttime in Chinese literature as a symbol of safety and visibility.

Proper Nouns

  • 康熙初年 - The early years of the Kangxi era.
  • 五通祠 - A shrine dedicated to the Five Lords.
  • 康熙 - The reign title of Emperor Kangxi of Qing, the fourth emperor of the Qing dynasty.

[1020]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 迨及其前 - Classical expression meaning 'when it came to the time before' or 'as it approached the time before.' Sets up a narrative progression.
  • 丐者皆群睨不舍 - Beggars all gazed at her without turning away or 'beggars all looked at her in a group without looking away.' Conveys a collective, lingering gaze, possibly with mockery.
  • 大有轻薄之色 - Displayed a great deal of disrespectful or flippant attitude. *轻薄* can mean 'flippant,' 'insolent,' or 'lascivious,' depending on context.
  • 妇益恐 - The woman became even more afraid. A simple expression of increasing fear.
  • 幸犹未措其手足 - Fortunately, they had not yet taken action with their hands and feet. Implies that the beggars had not physically touched her.
  • 只以唇舌相嘲笑 - Only with words and speech they mocked her. Indicates verbal mockery rather than physical aggression.
  • 听之而已 - She merely listened to it. A passive response to the mockery.
  • 归至家门，天已向晦 - When she returned to her home, the sky had already turned toward dusk. A classical way of saying 'it was already evening.'
  • 郁郁弗舒 - She was deeply distressed and unable to relax. *郁郁* conveys a state of deep melancholy or frustration.
  • 命小婢阖户早寝 - She ordered her young maid to close the door and go to sleep early. A formal command to a servant.
  • 乃妇方假寐 - But the woman was just pretending to sleep. *假寐* means to feign sleep or to be in a half-awake state.
  • 恍闻床头似有人聚语者 - She vaguely heard people gathered and speaking near her bed. *恍闻* suggests a faint or dreamlike hearing.
  • 请俟之他日 - Let us wait for another day. A polite request to delay an action.
  • 渠思嫁颇亟 - She wishes to marry very urgently. *渠* is a third-person pronoun, here referring to the woman.
  • 倘吏有夫，未识道里远迩 - If the official has a husband, we do not know how far or near the distance is. *道里* is a term for distance or route.
  • 盎据之于未嫁之先 - We will occupy her before she is married. *盎据* is a rare or archaic term, possibly meaning 'to occupy' or 'to take possession of.'
  • 渠固无能再嫁 - She certainly cannot remarry. A statement of inevitability.
  • 其音微细，才可辨识 - Their voices were very faint, barely discernible. A description of the sounds heard.
  • 察其非人 - She realized they were not human. *察* means to perceive or discern.
  • 心大惊 - She was greatly shocked. A strong emotional reaction.
  • 俄而声且孔扬 - Soon, the sound became very loud. *孔扬* is an intensifier meaning 'very loud' or 'extremely raised.'
  • 今夕姑逊大兄 - Tonight we will yield to the eldest brother. *逊* here may mean 'to yield' or 'to defer.'
  • 弟辈以齿为序 - The younger ones will follow in order of age. *齿* refers to age or seniority.
  • 自此美人之良宵不虑虚度矣 - From now on, this beautiful woman's good night will not be wasted. *良宵* is a poetic term for a beautiful night.
  • 如有飞隼一一破窗而去 - As if falcons one by one flew through the window and left. A metaphorical or supernatural description of the figures departing.
  • 心旌动摇，肢体俱软 - Her heart trembled, and her body became weak. A metaphor for emotional and physical vulnerability.
  • 亟呼婢 - She urgently called her maid. *亟* means 'hurriedly' or 'immediately.'
  • 深入黑甜 - She fell into a deep sleep. *黑甜* is a poetic term for deep, dreamless sleep.
  • 强起燃烛 - She forced herself to get up and light a candle. *强起* means to struggle to rise.
  • 室中阒其无人 - The room was empty of people. *阒* means quiet or empty.
  • 自以为梦境，或心虚所致耳 - She thought it was a dream, or perhaps caused by her nervousness. *心虚* can mean 'nervousness,' 'self-doubt,' or 'shyness.'
  • 因更整衣而卧 - She then dressed again and lay down. A return to normal routine after the disturbance.
  • 眠席未暖，早入睡乡 - The sleeping mat had not yet warmed, and she soon fell back into sleep. A description of quick re-sleeping.
  • 梦寐中顿觉下体有异 - In her dream, she suddenly felt something strange in her lower body. *下体* is a direct reference to the genital area.
  • 惊寤而视之 - She awoke in惊 (alarm) and looked at it. *寤* is the verb for waking up.
  • 独烛未息，向人眇而跛者，己裸然累乎其上 - Only the candle was still burning, and a person who was one-eyed and lame was already lying naked on top of her. *眇* is one-eyed, *跛* is lame, *裸然* is naked, *累乎其上* is 'lying on top of her.'
  • 妇大惭 - The woman was greatly ashamed. *大惭* is a strong expression of shame.
  • 自视其身，不第一丝不挂 - She looked at herself and saw that not a single thread of clothing remained. *不第一丝不挂* is a classical way of saying 'completely naked.'
  • 早且百体俱呈 - She was already completely exposed. *百体* refers to the entire body.
  • 益腼腆无以自容 - She became even more shy and had no way to bear it. *腼腆* is a term for shyness or modesty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丐者 - Beggars, a common social class in classical Chinese society, often associated with poverty or marginalization.
  • 妇 - 'The woman,' a term used in classical texts to refer to a female, often in the context of marriage or household.
  • 小婢 - 'A young maid,' a common servant in classical Chinese households.
  • 假寐 - 'Feigning sleep,' a practice used in classical literature to imply vigilance or pretense.
  • 飞隼 - 'Falcon,' a symbol of swiftness and danger, often used in classical literature to represent supernatural or malevolent forces.
  • 黑甜 - 'Deep, dreamless sleep,' a poetic term for deep rest or unconsciousness.
  • 心虚 - 'Nervousness' or 'shyness,' a term that can imply emotional or psychological vulnerability.
  • 百体俱呈 - 'All parts of the body revealed,' a metaphor for complete exposure or vulnerability.
  • 腼腆 - 'Shyness' or 'modesty,' a term often used to describe a woman's emotional state in classical literature.

[1021]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旋闻丐者附耳语曰 - Introduces the beggar and his whispered words, setting up the narrative.
  • 予实本境之福神也 - Claim of being a god of fortune.
  • 昨于祠前睹卿玉貌 - Saw your beautiful appearance at the temple.
  • 不觉倾倒 - Couldn't help but fall in love.
  • 愿假一夕之欢 - Wish to have one night's pleasure.
  • 卿其勿拒 - Please do not refuse.
  • 倘五者迭来 - If five come in succession.
  • 则身同传舍 - The body becomes a relay station.
  • 若能以计制之 - Use strategy to control them.
  • 退其一 - Drive away one.
  • 余者或不复逞 - The others may not continue.
  • 第鲜良图 - Only a good plan.
  • 庸讵能免 - How can avoid.
  • 神又伏轼撙衔 - Spirit lowers the carriage and holds the reins.
  • 极其驰骋 - Rides with all speed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 裴航 - Legendary figure in Chinese folklore, known for his romantic encounter with a fairy. His story is often used to symbolize unattainable love or supernatural romance.

  • 司马昭 - Historical figure from the Three Kingdoms period, known for his political cunning and ambition. The phrase '司马昭之心，路人皆知' (Sima Zhao's heart, even the common people know it) is a common idiom meaning that someone's true intentions are obvious.
  • 图南 - Idiom derived from the *Book of Changes* (Yijing), where a bird named '南' (Nan) symbolizes ambition and success.
  • 斥鷃 - Small bird, often used in classical Chinese literature to represent something insignificant or easily ridiculed.
  • 南宫 - Reference to the imperial examination halls, where scholars took the civil service exams.
  • 刘蕡 - Tang dynasty scholar who failed the imperial examinations, despite his talents. His story is often used as a cautionary tale about the pitfalls of rushing into important endeavors without proper preparation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Common phrase used to introduce a commentary or reflection, often attributed to an anonymous historian or sage.
  • 雅俗之分 - Refers to the distinction between refined and vulgar, or high and low culture.
  • 洛阳好女儿 - Literally 'good daughters of Luoyang,' referring to virtuous women from the city of Luoyang.
  • 河间荡妇 - Literally 'carefree women of Hejian,' referring to women from the region of Hejian who are described as promiscuous or morally loose.
  • 媚猪 - Metaphorical expression, possibly referring to a woman who is overly seductive or manipulative, like a pig that is attractive but not desirable.
  • 嘻笑 - Refers to laughter or jesting, often in a light-hearted or frivolous manner.
  • 婉丽 - Describes someone as graceful and beautiful.
  • 煽处特闻 - Literally 'known for stirring up trouble,' implying that the woman is notorious for causing scandal or mischief.
  • 醋炙娘子 - Colloquial term for a jealous or possessive woman, often used in a humorous or derogatory way.
  • 怒目于衾底之流芳 - Literally 'releasing fragrance from under the quilt with angry eyes,' a metaphor for a woman who is both beautiful and fierce.
  • 忘情于床头之作俑 - Literally 'forgetting emotion at the foot of the bed,' a metaphor for a woman who is sexually promiscuous.
  • 苏子瞻 - Reference to Su Shi (苏轼), a famous Song Dynasty poet and statesman.
  • 朝云 - Su Shi's beloved concubine, known for her beauty and talent.
  • 第一恨事 - Literally 'the greatest regret,' referring to the most significant sorrow or disappointment.
